start.speaker: elara
start.text: I've been thinking. The nightcap poisoning — I know a way to counteract it. There's a flower called moonpetal that grows deep in Thornwood. My grandmother taught me a recipe.

explain_recipe.speaker: elara
explain_recipe.text: Five sprigs of moonpetal, crushed and steeped in boiled rainwater for three hours. It binds to the nightcap toxin and neutralizes it. But moonpetal only grows in one place — a grove past the wolf territory.

options.text: Tell me where the grove is. I'll get the moonpetal.
Wolves? How dangerous are we talking?
How many are sick? Is this really worth the risk?
Can't we just buy the herbs from a trader?
I'm not risking my neck for herbs. Find someone else.

wolf_warning.speaker: elara
wolf_warning.text: A pack of eight or nine. They've been territorial since the forest started dying — protecting what little prey remains. They won't attack unless you enter their den area. The moonpetal grows right in the middle of it.

wolf_advice.speaker: elara
wolf_advice.text: You could try to sneak past them at dawn when they sleep, or... well, I have a mixture that masks human scent. It's not perfect, but it might give you an edge.

options.text: Give me the scent mask. I'll be careful.
I can handle wolves. Just point me to the grove.
Nine wolves is nine too many. Sorry, Elara.

sick_count.speaker: elara
sick_count.text: Three are critical. Old Thomas hasn't opened his eyes in two days. Young Bess can barely breathe. And Aldwin — he's only seven years old. Without the antidote, they'll be dead within the week.

options.text: A seven-year-old boy. I'll get your herbs, Elara.
Three lives for a trip into wolf territory. What's the pay?

payment_offer.speaker: elara
payment_offer.text: I don't have gold. But I can brew you healing salves that no apothecary can match. And... I know things about this village. Secrets that have value to someone like you.

no_traders.speaker: elara
no_traders.text: The Eastern Road is patrolled by Ironmarch. No traders have come through in weeks. And moonpetal doesn't keep — it has to be fresh, used within a day of picking. Thornwood is the only option.

grateful_directions.speaker: elara
grateful_directions.text: Thank you. The grove is about two hours into Thornwood, past the split oak. Look for a clearing where the canopy opens — moonpetal needs starlight to bloom. You'll know it by the silver petals.

final_warning.speaker: elara
final_warning.text: One more thing. Fenrick has been asking about rare Thornwood herbs. If he approaches you about the moonpetal... please. Don't sell it to him. People's lives depend on this.

disappointed.speaker: elara
disappointed.text: I understand. It's a lot to ask of a stranger. But if you change your mind... the sick can't wait much longer.
